steps.title: Find the decay multiplier
steps.content:
A decrease of $18\%$ means the sample keeps $82\%$ of its mass after each period.

$$100\%-18\%=82\%=0.82$$

steps.title: Count the periods
steps.content:
Each period is $11.4$ minutes, so after $t$ minutes, the number of decay periods is

$$\frac{t}{11.4}$$

steps.title: Build the model
steps.content: Use the kept fraction as the base and the number of periods as the exponent. $$M=100(0.82)^{\frac{t}{11.4}}$$ The answer is C.
